title: fix(release): resolve configured packages from workspace paths
summary: The PR correctly fixes the bug where configured `config.packages` entries always resolved to conventional `packages/{scope}/` paths, ignoring nonstandard workspace layouts. The strategy â€” scan first, match by moniker, fall back to inferred â€” is sound. Two real-filesystem regression tests cover the happy path and the stale-config case. One behavioral inconsistency (silent scan error swallowing) and two minor quality issues are noted.

findings.title: Scan failure is silently swallowed when config packages are present
Unnecessary `as Record<string, Package>` cast on `discoveredByName`
Duplicated test setup across both test cases
findings.body: Both tests create the same temp directory structure (`tooling/pkg-core` with `@kitz/core`). This could be extracted into a `beforeEach`/`afterEach` pair with a shared `rootDir` variable, reducing ~25 lines of duplicate setup and making future additions cheaper.

residual_risks: The inferred fallback path `packages/{scope}/` is not validated to exist, so stale config entries produce a `Package` object with a non-existent `path`. Downstream consumers must handle missing paths gracefully.
The `discoveredByName` map uses moniker as key â€” if two workspace packages share the same moniker (degenerate repo), the last one wins silently.
